title: Dissochaeta stipularis
taxonomy: Dissochaeta stipularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the melastome family. This family comprises approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Dissochaeta stipularis specifically belongs to the genus Dissochaeta, which is a group of plants that are mostly endemic to Southeast Asia. The classification of Dissochaeta stipularis is further supported by molecular and morphological studies that have helped to clarify its position within the plant kingdom. Further research may continue to refine the classification of Dissochaeta stipularis as more information becomes available.
